findings.message: The scope count is still off. Direct grep for the three target DS rules returns 22 file-level BRIK-3 suppressions plus 3 inline suppressions across 24 unique files; the brief still describes 24 file-level files and repeats that overcount in the outcome, inventory, blast-radius, and acceptance wording.

The testing guidance no longer matches the corrected rule semantics. This line, the recommended test approach at line 205, and the confidence note at line 282 still treat ds/min-tap-size as satisfiable by min-h-10 or h-10 alone.
The default-scope recommendation reintroduces HomePage.tsx as an inline BRIK-3 cleanup target even though line 138 correctly classifies HomePage.tsx:151 as BRIK-002 and out of scope.

The delivery-readiness uplift is stale. The brief already established that @acme/design-system/primitives does not export Page/Section/Container/Overlay and that Prime's local Container component is the intended seam.

The rule-specific remediation guidance is materially stronger than before. It now anchors container-width fixes on Prime's local Container component and correctly rejects the earlier min-h-10-only interpretation of ds/min-tap-size.
